perturbation.original_text: 4. Compensation. In consideration of the services to be rendered by Maimon hereunder and for Maimon's service as a director on the Board of Directors of the Company, the Company will pay Maimon a monthly fee of $1,000 during the Minimum Period, beginning on the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Compensation. In consideration of the services to be rendered by Maimon hereunder and for Maimon's service as a director on the Board of Directors of the Company, the Company will pay Maimon a monthly fee of $1,000 during the first 6 months of the Minimum Period, beginning on the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. Section 4 initially states that Maimon will be paid $1,000 per month during the 'Minimum Period'. By modifying it to state '$1,000 during the first 6 months of the Minimum Period', it now contradicts Section 3 which defines the Minimum Period as 12 months. This creates uncertainty about Maimon's compensation for the remaining 6 months of the Minimum Period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Term. Subject to the provisions for termination hereinafter provided, the term of this Agreement shall commence on the date hereof (the "Effective Date") and shall continue for a minimum period of 12 months (the "Minimum Period") and thereafter upon the mutual agreement of the Company and Maimon (the "Service Term").
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the compensation term from "during the Minimum Period" to "during the first 6 months of the Minimum Period". This directly contradicts Section 3, which defines the "Minimum Period" as 12 months. Consequently, there's a clear inconsistency regarding Maimon's compensation for the remaining 6 months of the minimum period, as the changed text implies they won't be paid during that time despite the agreement defining a 12-month term.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Termination. If Maimon should become unable to serve as CEO, or should fail to perform any of the obligations hereunder for any cause including death or disability, always in the sole judgment and decision of the Company, then the Company shall have the right to terminate this agreement on five days prior written notice. Maimon shall have the right to resign at any time upon 30 days prior written notice. The Company may in its discretion and at its option terminate this Agreement at any time after the Minimum Period upon five days prior written notice to Maimon.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Termination. If Maimon should become unable to serve as CEO, or should fail to perform any of the obligations hereunder for any cause including death or disability, always in the sole judgment and decision of the Company, then the Company shall have the right to terminate this agreement on five days prior written notice. Maimon shall have the right to resign at any time upon 30 days prior written notice. The Company may in its discretion and at its option terminate this Agreement at any time after the Minimum Period upon thirty days prior written notice to Maimon.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the notice period for termination after the Minimum Period. Originally, the company could terminate with five days' notice. Now, after the change, the notice period is thirty days. This contradicts the original five-day notice period specified in the same Section 5 and creates ambiguity over which notice period applies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Termination. If Maimon should become unable to serve as CEO, or should fail to perform any of the obligations hereunder for any cause including death or disability, always in the sole judgment and decision of the Company, then the Company shall have the right to terminate this agreement on five days prior written notice. Maimon shall have the right to resign at any time upon 30 days prior written notice. The Company may in its discretion and at its option terminate this Agreement at any time after the Minimum Period upon thirty days prior written notice to Maimon.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the notice period required for the Company to terminate the agreement after the Minimum Period from five days to thirty days. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, now specifies thirty days. However, the first sentence of the same section states that the Company can terminate the agreement on five days prior written notice if Maimon is unable to serve as CEO or fails to perform any of the obligations. This creates an inconsistency because there are now two different notice periods for termination by the Company, creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties'.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Conflicts of Interest, Non-Competition, Non-Solicitation. Maimon agrees during the term of this Agreement not to accept work or enter into a contract or accept an obligation inconsistent or incompatible with Maimon's obligations under this Agreement or with the scope of services to be rendered for the Company. Maimon warrants that to the best of Maimon's knowledge, there is no other contract or duty on Maimon's part now in existence inconsistent with this Agreement. During the term of this Agreement and for a period of two (2) years after expiration or termination for any reason of this Agreement, Maimon agrees not to: (a) compete with the business of the Company, whether individually or through any entity, or to use (or permit the use of) any Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, for the purpose of competing with the business of the Company; or (b) suggest to, induce or persuade any customer, client, vendor, supplier, employee, consultant or agent of the Company to terminate or diminish its relationship with the Company..
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Conflicts of Interest, Non-Competition, Non-Solicitation. Maimon agrees during the term of this Agreement not to accept work or enter into a contract or accept an obligation inconsistent or incompatible with Maimon's obligations under this Agreement or with the scope of services to be rendered for the Company. Maimon warrants that to the best of Maimon's knowledge, there is no other contract or duty on Maimon's part now in existence inconsistent with this Agreement. During the term of this Agreement and for a period of one (1) year after expiration or termination for any reason of this Agreement, Maimon agrees not to: (a) compete with the business of the Company, whether individually or through any entity, or to use (or permit the use of) any Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, for the purpose of competing with the business of the Company; or (b) suggest to, induce or persuade any customer, client, vendor, supplier, employee, consultant or agent of the Company to terminate or diminish its relationship with the Company..
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the duration of the non-competition clause. Originally, Maimon was restricted from competing for two years after the agreement's termination. By changing this to one year, a direct contradiction is created within the same section. Now it's unclear whether the non-compete period is one year or two years, leading to uncertainty in enforcement and potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Conflicts of Interest, Non-Competition, Non-Solicitation. Maimon agrees during the term of this Agreement not to accept work or enter into a contract or accept an obligation inconsistent or incompatible with Maimon's obligations under this Agreement or with the scope of services to be rendered for the Company. Maimon warrants that to the best of Maimon's knowledge, there is no other contract or duty on Maimon's part now in existence inconsistent with this Agreement. During the term of this Agreement and for a period of one (1) year after expiration or termination for any reason of this Agreement, Maimon agrees not to: (a) compete with the business of the Company, whether individually or through any entity, or to use (or permit the use of) any Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, for the purpose of competing with the business of the Company; or (b) suggest to, induce or persuade any customer, client, vendor, supplier, employee, consultant or agent of the Company to terminate or diminish its relationship with the Company..
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the duration of the non-competition agreement from two years to one year. The contradicted text also states the non-compete lasts for one year, which is the same as the Changed text. However, the Perturbation Explanation section states: "By changing this to one year, a direct contradiction is created within the same section. Now it's unclear whether the non-compete period is one year or two years, leading to uncertainty in enforcement and potential disputes." The original text had it for two years, while both the changed and contradicted texts have it for one year. This leads to an inconsistency and uncertainty about the duration of the non-compete clause, thus creating a direct in-text contradiction under the definition of "Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction".
